Notes: PPP-led coalition formed after Dec 2007 election.
Opposition filed no-confidence motion in late June. Government survived.
Samak removed by court Sep 9 (cooking show). NOT a no-confidence event.
Somchai took over from Samak. PAD airport seizures Nov.
PPP dissolved by court Dec 2. Coalition collapsed. Mass defection to form Abhisit govt.
First no-confidence motion filed by Puea Thai. Abhisit survived by ~53 votes.
Stable period. Red Shirt protests in Apr dispersed.
Red Shirt protests escalating but no parliamentary threat.
No-confidence debate. Survived. Post-crackdown period.
Third no-confidence motion. Survived. Margin thin.
House dissolved May 10. Election called for July.
Pheu Thai won landslide. 6-party coalition. Strong majority.
Stable. No significant threats.
No-confidence debate Nov 26-28. Yingluck survived easily with 3/5 majority.
Stable. Rice pledging scheme controversy building.
No-confidence filed over amnesty bill. Anti-govt protests began. Yingluck survived vote.
House dissolved Dec 9. Caretaker government.
Caretaker. Feb election boycotted, annulled by court.
Military junta. No parliament. N/A for survival probability.
Military junta. No parliament.
Military junta. Pre-election.
Elected PM via Senate vote. Razor-thin margin in House.
No-confidence debate Feb. Prayuth survived. FFP dissolved same month.
Gained seats from FFP dissolution fallout. Protests building.
No-confidence motion Feb. Survived.
Another no-confidence. Survived but margin eroding.
No-confidence debate Jul 18-22. Survived. Last censure before term expiry.
Suspended by court Aug (term limit). Returned Sep after favorable ruling.
11-party coalition formed. Large majority. MFP in opposition.
Stable. Some minor coalition friction but no defections.
Approval declining (12.85%) but coalition holding.
Took over from Srettha. MFP dissolved Aug 7. Coalition held.
Thamanat faction (19 MPs) expelled from PPRP Dec. Coalition gradually shifting.

Border skirmish May 28. Tensions rising with Bhumjaithai over Interior Ministry.
Hun Sen phone call leaked Jun 18. Bhumjaithai (69 seats) withdrew Jun 19. No-confidence announced Jun 24. Coalition lost majority.
Paetongtarn suspended by court Jul 1. Remaining 6 parties held at ~254 seats. Caretaker Phumtham.
Paetongtarn removed Aug 29. Government collapsed. People's Party backed Anutin.
Minority govt. People's Party confidence and supply. Required to dissolve House within 4 months.
House dissolved Dec 12. Caretaker PM. Election Feb 8 2026.
